verses.text: Moses gathered all Israel: these are the words the LORD commanded. Six days work; the seventh shall be holy, a sabbath of rest — whoever works dies. Kindle no fire in your habitations on the sabbath day. Guidance: Before any tabernacle wood is cut, sabbath is named again. Building God's house cannot break the rhythm God built into creation. Even fire-kindling — the most basic task of the home — pauses for Him.

Moses gathered all Israel: these are the words the LORD commanded. Six days work; the seventh shall be holy, a sabbath of rest — whoever works dies. Kindle no fire in your habitations on the sabbath day. Guidance: Before any tabernacle wood is cut, sabbath is named again. Building God's house cannot break the rhythm God built into creation. Moses: the LORD commanded — take an offering. Whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring: gold, silver, brass, blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair, rams' skins dyed red, badgers' skins, shittim wood, oil for light, spices for anointing and incense, onyx stones, stones for ephod and breastplate. Guidance: God could have rained tabernacle materials from heaven. Instead He waits to see what Israel will choose to give. Willingness is what He builds with — not first the gold.

Wise hearted, come and make all the LORD commanded — tabernacle, tent, covering; ark, mercy seat, vail; table, vessels, shewbread; candlestick, lamps, oil; incense altar; anointing oil and incense; door hanging; altar, grate; laver and foot; court hangings, pillars, sockets; pins, cords; holy garments for Aaron and his sons. Guidance: A full inventory before a single nail is hammered. The wise-hearted hear the list, see the whole, then begin. God's work begins by seeing what the work actually is.

Israel departed. Everyone whose heart stirred brought offering. Men and women came with earrings and jewels of gold. Every one with blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, rams' and badgers' skins. Every one of silver, brass, shittim wood. Wise-hearted women spun blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair. Rulers brought onyx, spice, oil. Every heart made willing brought offering. Guidance: Men brought what they earned. Women brought what they spun. Rulers brought what they led. God's house is built by every hand willing to give its own.

Moses: the LORD has called by name Bezaleel of Judah, filled with the Spirit of God — wisdom, understanding, knowledge, all workmanship in gold, silver, brass, stones, wood. He has put in his heart to teach — he and Aholiab of Dan. Filled to work — engraver, cunning workman, embroiderer, weaver. Guidance: God's craftsmen are also called to teach. The Spirit fills hands to make, then fills hearts to pass the craft along. Holy skill is never meant to die with one generation.
